33.reason: The loop only iterates once; after extending left or right, segments that could connect to the new endpoints and appear earlier in the array are missed. A while loop or multiple passes are needed to correctly build the entire chain.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop only iterates once; after extending left or right, segments that could connect to the new endpoints and appear earlier in the array are missed. A while loop or multiple passes are needed to correctly build the entire chain.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The inner loop only scans once; after finding a match and updating an endpoint, it does not restart scanning, so segments that appear earlier in the array but match the new endpoint will be missed. A while loop or a repeated scan is needed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The inner loop only iterates once over all segments, failing to extend the chain beyond length 2. It needs to repeatedly search for connected segments until no more are found. Additionally, checking backward connections contradicts the strictly increasing x-coordinate constraint.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop only scans the array once, which fails to chain multiple segments sequentially when they are not in order. It should repeatedly search for the next connected segment until no further connections are found.

41.reason: [The logic incorrectly allows extending the chain backward and forward in the same pass, which may count segments inconsistently and violate the requirement that x-coordinates must be strictly increasing across the entire continuous segment. Also, after extending backward, the starting point changes but subsequent forward extensions from the new start are not explored in the same iteration.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  Extending backwards violates the constraint that x-coordinates must be strictly increasing along the continuous line. Only forward extension should be allowed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The logic incorrectly allows extending the chain backward and forward in the same pass, which may count segments inconsistently and violate the requirement that x-coordinates must be strictly increasing across the entire continuous segment. Also, after extending backward, the starting point changes but subsequent forward extensions from the new start are not explored in the same iteration.]
